topic.title_zh: PD-1 Inhibitors Plus Lenvatinib Versus Lenvatinib Alone for Unresectable Hepatocellular Carcinoma: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
topic.title_en: PD-1 Inhibitors Plus Lenvatinib Versus Lenvatinib Alone for Unresectable HCC: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
topic.background: Unresectable hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) has a poor prognosis; lenvatinib monotherapy is one standard first-line option. In recent years, multiple RCTs have explored PD-1 inhibitors combined with lenvatinib, but their conclusions are inconsistent; evidence synthesis is needed to guide clinical decision-making.

pico.population: Adults (>=18 years) with histologically or radiologically confirmed unresectable HCC, BCLC stage B-C, Child-Pugh A, no prior systemic therapy; exclude active HBV/HCV, HIV, other malignancy history.
pico.intervention: PD-1 inhibitor (pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w / nivolumab 240 mg q2w / tislelizumab 200 mg q3w) combined with lenvatinib (>=60 kg: 12 mg qd; <60 kg: 8 mg qd), treated until PD/toxicity/24 months.
pico.comparator: Lenvatinib monotherapy (same dose), or sorafenib 400 mg bid.
pico.research_question: In adults with unresectable HCC (BCLC B-C, Child-Pugh A, treatment-naive), does PD-1 inhibitor combined with lenvatinib compared with lenvatinib monotherapy improve OS and PFS?

meta_type.rationale: Intervention and comparator are both fixed two-arm comparisons with a simple evidence network; no NMA needed; all data are aggregate; no IPD needed.

methodological_details.Effect size: Time-to-event outcomes use HR + 95% CI; binary outcomes use RR + 95% CI
methodological_details.Synthesis model: Random-effects model (DerSimonian-Laird), due to expected high heterogeneity
methodological_details.Heterogeneity assessment: I2, tau2, Q test; I2 > 50% triggers subgroup analysis
methodological_details.Publication bias: When >=10 studies are included, Egger test + funnel plot + Trim-and-fill

clinical_value.reason: Directly addresses first-line treatment controversy; may change NCCN/CSCO guideline recommendations. Matches anchor 5: directly addresses a guideline-level controversy; expected to change clinical practice

methodological_feasibility.reason: Standard methods directly applicable; note potential heterogeneity across PD-1 inhibitors. Matches anchor 4: light methodological adaptation needed, but mature toolchain

data_availability.reason: Pre-search estimates 6-8 RCTs; outcomes mostly consistently reported. Matches anchor 4: 6-10 studies; outcomes mostly consistent

novelty.reason: A same-topic meta-analysis was published in 2024; must clearly state the new-study increment. Matches anchor 3: prior meta-analysis <3 years old, but with a clear evidence increment (pending Stage 4 dedup confirmation)

key_hits.title: PD-1 + Lenvatinib in HCC: A Meta-analysis

non_english_db.query: (PD-1 OR pembrolizumab OR sintilimab) AND (lenvatinib) AND (HCC OR hepatocellular carcinoma) AND ('Meta' OR 'systematic review')

key_hits.title: PD-1 + lenvatinib for advanced HCC: a Meta-analysis

near_duplicates.condition: Justify within-class substitution clinically + subgroup by PD-1/PD-L1 type

novelty_judgment.increment_sufficiency: Sufficient (>=2 new phase III RCTs added after 2024)

presearch.pubmed_query: (PD-1 OR pembrolizumab OR nivolumab OR tislelizumab) AND lenvatinib AND (HCC OR hepatocellular carcinoma)

prisma.overall_risk: MEDIUM (two-reviewer screening and equity dimension need shoring up)

prespecified.rationale: Different PD-1 inhibitors may differ in efficacy
Stage B vs stage C populations may benefit differently
HBV-dominant vs HCV-dominant populations may differ

sensitivity: Re-pool including only phase III RCTs

risks.mitigation: Subgroup by PD-1 type; pool separately if needed
Extend gray-literature search; wait for new RCTs if needed
Bring in a methodologist or a second reviewer

next_steps: Finalize the search strategy and have a methodologist review it
Draft the full protocol and have the team review it
Execute the three-database search and two-reviewer screening
